title: SplitFaceFeature Object
description: Object that represents an existing split face feature in a design.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the feature. This works for both parametric and non-parametric features.

methods.name: dissolve
methods.description: Dissolves the feature so that the feature information is lost and only the B-Rep geometry defined by the feature remains. This is only valid for non-parametric features.

methods.name: setAsAlongVectorSplitType
methods.description: Sets the split type to project the splitting tool along the direction defined by the specified entity.   To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

methods.name: setAsClosestPointSplitType
methods.description: Sets the split type to be a curve that defined by projecting the splitting curve to the closest point on the surface.   To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

methods.name: setAsSurfaceIntersectionSplitType
methods.description: Set the split type to be a surface to surface intersection. If the split tool is a curve it will be extruded into a surface to use in the split. If it's a surface, the surface will be used and optionally extended to fully intersect the faces to be split.   To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this face.

properties.name: baseFeature
properties.description: If this feature is associated with a base feature, this property will return that base feature. If it's not associated with a base feature, this property will return null.

properties.name: bodies
properties.description: Returns the bodies that were modified or created by this feature. This property works for both parametric and non-parametric features.   For a BaseFeature, this property has two different behaviors depending on if the BaseFeature is active for edit or not. When the base feature is active, this property returns the bodies owned by the base feature or the source bodies. When the base feature is not active, this property returns the result bodies.   When a body is added to a base feature, that body is owned by the base feature and is only seen in the UI when the base feature is active. This body is referred to as a "source body". Fusion creates a parametric copy of the body when you exit the base feature. This copy is referred to as the "result body," and it is used for subsequent modeling operations.   You can map between the source and result bodies by using their position within the bodies returned. To get a valid list of result bodies, you should roll the timeline to immediately after the base feature node in the timeline. Otherwise, subsequent operations could have done something to cause one or more bodies to no longer be available.

properties.name: directionEntity
properties.description: Gets the direction entity when the split type is along a vector. If the split type is not alongVectorSplitType this property will return null.   To set the direction entity use the setAsAlongVectorSplitType method.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the Feature object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same feature.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: errorOrWarningMessage
properties.description: Returns the error or warning message in the case where the healthState property returns either WarningFeatureHealthState or ErrorFeatureHealthState. Otherwise this property returns an empty string.

properties.name: faces
properties.description: Returns the faces that were created by this feature. This works for both parametric and non-parametric features.   Depending on the modifications made to the body after the feature was created, this may not return all of the faces. For example, when a component is created from a body, the original body and its faces no longer exist, so this won't return any faces. However, you can roll the timeline to immediately after the feature, and all the original faces will be available.

properties.name: facesToSplit
properties.description: Gets and sets the faces to be split. The collection can contain one or more faces selected from solid and/or open bodies.   To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

properties.name: healthState
properties.description: Returns the current health state of the feature.

properties.name: isParametric
properties.description: Indicates if this feature is parametric or not.

properties.name: isSplittingToolExtended
properties.description: Gets whether or not the setting to automatically extend the splittingTool was set when the feature was created.   This property is valid only when the splitType property returns surfaceIntersectionSplitType.

properties.name: isSuppressed
properties.description: Gets and sets if this feature is suppressed. This is only valid for parametric features.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: linkedFeatures
properties.description: Returns the set of features that are linked to this feature. The set of linked features are all of the features that were created in various components as the result of a single feature being created in the user interface.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Returns the name of the feature as seen in the browser (non-parametric) or in the timeline (parametric).

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly and in the context of its parent component. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentComponent
properties.description: Returns the parent component that owns this feature.

properties.name: splittingTool
properties.description: Gets the entity(s) that define the splitting tool(s). The splitting tool can consist of one or more of the following: BRepBody, ConstructionPlane, BRepFace, sketch curve that extends or can be extended beyond the extents of the face. To set the splitting tool, use one of the set methods to also define the split type.   To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

properties.name: splitType
properties.description: Returns the type of split type currently defined. To change the split type, use one of the set methods.

properties.name: timelineObject
properties.description: Returns the timeline object associated with this feature.

related_samples.name: Split Face Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new split face feature.

assemblyContext.title: SplitFaceFeature.assemblyContext Property
assemblyContext.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = splitFaceFeature_var.assemblyContext

classType.title: SplitFaceFeature.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.SplitFaceFeature.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::SplitFaceFeature::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createForAssemblyContext.title: SplitFaceFeature.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

syntax.python: returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var.createForAssemblyContext(occurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var->createForAssemblyContext(occurrence);

createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the proxy object or null if this is not the NativeObject.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that defines the context to create the proxy in.

deleteMe.title: SplitFaceFeature.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes the feature. This works for both parametric and non-parametric features.

syntax.python: returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns a bool indicating if the delete was successful or not.

dissolve.title: SplitFaceFeature.dissolve Method
dissolve.description: Dissolves the feature so that the feature information is lost and only the B-Rep geometry defined by the feature remains. This is only valid for non-parametric features.

syntax.python: returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var.dissolve()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var->dissolve();

dissolve.return_description: Returns a bool indicating if the dissolve was successful or not.

setAsAlongVectorSplitType.title: SplitFaceFeature.setAsAlongVectorSplitType Method
setAsAlongVectorSplitType.description: Sets the split type to project the splitting tool along the direction defined by the specified entity. To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

syntax.python: returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var.setAsAlongVectorSplitType(splittingTool, directionEntity)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var->setAsAlongVectorSplitType(splittingTool, directionEntity);

setAsAlongVectorSplitType.return_description: Returns true is setting the split type was successful.

parameters.name: splittingTool
parameters.description: Input entity(s) that defines the splitting tool. The splitting tool can be a single entity or an ObjectCollection containing faces or sketch curves. If faces are input, the edges of the face are used as the splitting tool.

parameters.name: directionEntity
parameters.description: An entity that defines the direction of projection of the splitting tool. This can be a linear BRepEdge, SketchLine, ConstructionLine, or a planar face where the face normal is used.

setAsClosestPointSplitType.title: SplitFaceFeature.setAsClosestPointSplitType Method
setAsClosestPointSplitType.description: Sets the split type to be a curve that defined by projecting the splitting curve to the closest point on the surface. To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

syntax.python: returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var.setAsClosestPointSplitType(splittingTool)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var->setAsClosestPointSplitType(splittingTool);

setAsClosestPointSplitType.return_description: Returns true if setting the closest point split type was successful.

parameters.name: splittingTool
parameters.description: Input entity(s) that defines the splitting tool. The splitting tool can be a single entity or an ObjectCollection containing faces or sketch curves. If faces are input, the edges of the face are used as the splitting tool.

setAsSurfaceIntersectionSplitType.title: SplitFaceFeature.setAsSurfaceIntersectionSplitType Method
setAsSurfaceIntersectionSplitType.description: Set the split type to be a surface to surface intersection. If the split tool is a curve it will be extruded into a surface to use in the split. If it's a surface, the surface will be used and optionally extended to fully intersect the faces to be split. To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

syntax.python: returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var.setAsSurfaceIntersectionSplitType(splittingTool, isSplittingToolExtended)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = splitFaceFeature_var->setAsSurfaceIntersectionSplitType(splittingTool, isSplittingToolExtended);

setAsSurfaceIntersectionSplitType.return_description: Returns true is setting the split type was successful.

parameters.name: splittingTool
parameters.description: Input entity(s) that defines the splitting tool. The splitting tool can be a single entity or an ObjectCollection containing solid and/or open bodies, construction planes, faces, or sketch curves that partially or fully intersect the faces that are being split.

parameters.name: isSplittingToolExtended
parameters.description: Specifies if the splitting tool should be extended so that is fully intersects the faces to be split.
